Class EfsIntervalEndpoint

java.lang.Object
org.efs.feed.EfsIntervalEndpoint
Direct Known Subclasses:
EfsDurationEndpoint, EfsIndexEndpoint, EfsTimeEndpoint
@Immutable public abstract class EfsIntervalEndpoint extends Object
An EfsInterval consists of two endpoints: beginning and ending. These end points are defined as either index or time. An index endpoint specifies a number of events. For example, a beginning endpoint may be for the up to 100 events in the past and and ending endpoint may be fore up to 100 events on into the future.

A time endpoint specifies events starting or stopping at particular time. However, it is possible to create a time interval based on a Duration and current time. For example, a beginning endpoint may be created for 5 minutes prior to the current time and an ending endpoint for 5 hours after the current time.

Endpoints have an associated EfsIntervalEndpoint.Clusivity. The interval may include or exclude the specified value. An index beginning endpoint with a initial value of 100 may be inclusive (interval starts at 1000 events in the past) or exclusive (interval starts at 99 events in the past).

Endpoints also have an interval location based on whether it is located in the past, now, future, or is on-going (that is, interval never terminates).

Author:
Charles W. Rapp

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    EfsIntervalEndpoint.Clusivity
    Specifies whether an end point is inclusive or exclusive.
    protected static class 
    EfsIntervalEndpoint.EndpointBuilder<T extends EfsIntervalEndpoint>
    Base class for all endpoint builders.
    static enum 
    EfsIntervalEndpoint.EndpointType
    An endpoint is based either on fixed time, time offset, or index offset.
    static enum 
    EfsIntervalEndpoint.IntervalLocation
    Denotes if endpoint denotes past, current time, future, or future on-going.
    static enum 
    EfsIntervalEndpoint.IntervalSide
    Specifies on which interval side this endpoint is placed.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Returns true if o is an EfsIntervalEndpoint instance with equal EfsIntervalEndpoint.Clusivity and EfsIntervalEndpoint.IntervalLocation as this EfsIntervalEndpoint instance.

      equals in class Object

      o - comparison argument.

    • toString

      public final String toString(EfsIntervalEndpoint.IntervalSide side)
      Outputs this endpoint in format appropriate to which side of the interval the endpoint is on.
      Parameters:
      side - interval side.
      Returns:
      endpoint as text.

    • compareEndpoints

      public static int compareEndpoints(@Nonnull EfsIntervalEndpoint ep0, @Nonnull EfsIntervalEndpoint ep1)
      Returns integer value <, equal to, or > zero if ep0 is <, equal to, > ep1. Comparison is based on EfsIntervalEndpoint.IntervalLocation and, if zero, then on EfsIntervalEndpoint.Clusivity.
      Parameters:
      ep0 - first endpoint.
      ep1 - second endpoint.
      Returns:
      integer value <, equal to, or > zero.
